title: Small passenger ship
content:
{{Top|leg}}
The term '''small passenger ship''' (or '''SSP''', for "small starship, passenger") was the general designation for [[Passenger liner/Legends|passenger liners]] less than 100 meters long, carrying up to 500 passengers.

The precise definition of the classification varied somewhat in different [[Sector/Legends|sectors]], but apart from the basic constraints of size and capacity, the only limit that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] placed on the SSP classification was that a ship had to operate under a license from the [[Governor/Legends|governor]] on the specific [[Planet/Legends|planet]] where it claimed its home port. The planetary administration was responsible for ensuring that the ship satisfied Imperial safety regulations, and also for levying appropriate fees and dues from it.

Some planets were known for offering cheap licenses and lax regulations—and some SSPs were known to hold licenses from multiple home ports: by presenting a license from another port, the ship's [[Captain/Legends|captain]] could permanently avoid fulfilling his responsibilities.

Due to the basic limits on the designation, the SSP category included almost every small ship with a passenger license, whether it was a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], or [[Star Cruiser/Legends|star cruiser]]. They were, however, divided between "line-operated" ships and "independents." Lines typically provided regular inter-system services within a single [[Sector/Legends|sector]]; independents included every individual captain whose ship was licensed to carry passengers, with all the variation that implied—though they rarely pursued a fixed schedule of routes.

==Sources==
*<!-- 1987-11-XX -->''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''
